summary: Cleaned up src/melee/it/items/it_27CF.c and matched the two remaining it_27CF animation functions. The diff removes disabled alternate code and stale signature comments, reduces redundant casts around item helpers, uses GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ where possible, and keeps narrow matching steering with PAD_STACK(10) in the two newly matched animation functions. A bot report records two new matches for it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; item behavior cleanup and matching work for it_27CF functions.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: 'Matched the 2 remaining functions... general cleanup'

what_changed.text: Matched the two remaining functions according to the PR body and bot report: it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim.
Removed a disabled #if 0 alternate inline/helper implementation block and stale commented prototype lines around several it_27CF functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removal of '#if 0' block containing inline1 and alternate functions

what_changed.text: Simplified redundant casts in helper calls, for example GET_ITEM(item_gobj), it_802F3020(item_gobj), it_8026B3A8(item_gobj), Item_80268E5C(item_gobj,...), and GET_JOBJ(item_gobj).
Replaced several direct item_gobj->user_data item accesses with GET_ITEM(item_gobj) or GET_ITEM(gobj), while a few direct user_data accesses remain where the matching shape uses them.
Changed the two animation functions to take parameter name gobj, use Item* ip, introduce an Item_GObj* item_gobj local for the final it_802F3290 call, and retain PAD_STACK(10).
evidence_refs: raw_diff: Item* ip; Item_GObj* item_gobj; PAD_STACK(10)

what_changed.text: Simplified damage-received lifetime update by removing a commented alternate expression and keeping item->xD44_lifeTimer -= item->xCA0 * item->xDD4_itemVar.it_27CF.x0.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: it_27CF_Logic114_DmgReceived removed commented negated expression

smart_moves.text: The cleanup narrows the source to the active matching implementation by deleting disabled alternative code and stale comments, reducing review noise in the item file.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: deleted #if 0/#else wrapper and alternate inline1 block
changed_files: 100 deleted lines vs 38 added lines

smart_moves.text: The author used typed helper macros for item and jobj access where possible instead of repeated raw user_data access or unnecessary casts.
The PR kept the matching-sensitive PAD_STACK tactic localized to the two animation functions that the bot report identifies as newly matched.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(10) in it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim
text_corpus issue_comment: two new matches are those same functions

decomp_lessons.lesson: In item code, replacing direct user_data item retrieval with GET_ITEM is a supported cleanup when the helper expresses the same typed access and does not disturb the matching shape.
Remove disabled alternate decomp attempts and stale signature comments once a matching source shape lands, to keep the file focused on reviewable source.
For matching-sensitive item animation functions, a small local stack tactic such as PAD_STACK can be retained when paired with verifier evidence showing the affected functions reached 100%.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: PAD_STACK(10) in both animation functions
text_corpus issue_comment: bot report lists both functions as 100.00% after

naming_conventions.convention: Use short local role names such as gobj for a generic HSD_GObj parameter and ip for an Item* local when the role is clear in item functions.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: item_gobj parameter renamed to gobj in it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim
raw_diff: Item* item changed to Item* ip in those functions

naming_conventions.convention: Preserve address-style function names for unresolved it_27CF helpers rather than inventing semantic names.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: functions remain named it_802F3020, it_802F317C, it_802F3290, it_27CF_UnkMotion*_Anim/Phys/Coll

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Permutation was used by the author to match the two remaining functions and some others, but the slice does not show the exact permutation attempts.
PAD_STACK(10) remained in it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim, and the bot report records both as newly matched.
The matching shape in both animation functions keeps a mixture of GET_ITEM(gobj), direct gobj->user_data, a saved item_jobj local, and a casted Item_GObj* local before it_802F3290.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev[bot] reported matched code 53.71% (+0.01%, +336 bytes) and two new matches in main/melee/it/items/it_27CF: it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim.
review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]

follow_up_queries: GET_ITEM instead of user_data item cleanup

raw_diff: complete diff for one changed file
text_corpus.jsonl: bot report with match table
notes: No human review comments were present in the loaded slice; review_feedback is limited to the bot report.
The PR body says functions were permuted, but the slice does not include permutation logs or exact attempt history.
The bot report supports the two new function matches, but no local build command, objdiff command, or adjacent regression details are shown in the slice.
PAD_STACK use is visible and correlated with newly matched functions, but the slice does not independently prove why PAD_STACK(10) was required.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use GET_ITEM/GET_JOBJ helpers to clean item object access when type-compatible
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2193 cleaned it_27CF item code by replacing several direct user_data accesses and redundant HSD_GObj casts with GET_ITEM or GET_JOBJ helper usage. This aligns with typed access cleanup, while the diff also shows that some direct user_data access may remain when needed for the matching shape.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PAD_STACK can remain narrowly when tied to specific newly matched functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2193 retained PAD_STACK(10) only in it_27CF_UnkMotion1_Anim and it_27CF_UnkMotion0_Anim; the bot report lists exactly those two functions as new 100% matches. Treat this as a narrow tactic with evidence, not a general style rule.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Delete disabled alternate decomp blocks after landing the matching implementation
accepted_candidate_records.text: The PR removed a disabled #if 0 alternate inline/helper implementation and stale commented prototypes from it_27CF.c, reducing review noise after matching progress.

source_update_candidates.title: Clarify helper macros over raw item user_data access
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding item-specific wording under typed-fields-over-pointer-math: when working with item GObjs, prefer established helper macros such as GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ over direct user_data/hsd_obj access or redundant casts where the helper expresses the typed operation and matching evidence allows it.
source_update_candidates.reason: The behavior is already broadly covered by typed-field standards, but this PR gives item-specific evidence that helper macros are preferred cleanup where possible.

source_update_candidates.title: Emphasize deleting disabled alternate generated/decomp attempts after a match lands
source_update_candidates.text: Consider noting that disabled #if 0 alternate decomp attempts and stale prototype comments should be removed once the reviewable matching implementation is present, unless they document unresolved verifier evidence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed disabled alternate inline1/functions block
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete cleanup example, but a curator should decide whether this belongs in standards or remains a local lesson.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that every user_data access in item code should be converted to GET_ITEM; this diff leaves direct user_data in the two animation functions where the matching shape appears to need it.
Do not promote PAD_STACK(10) as a general tactic for item animation functions; evidence only supports its narrow use in the two newly matched functions in this PR.
Do not claim human reviewer approval rationale; the loaded slice has no human review comments.
